CHAR-001.emotionalState: Numb endurance with hypervigilant threat assessment — chronic pain accepted as deserved, sudden reactivation of command instincts when surveillance detected
CHAR-001.trigger: Oathbrand flare, Pallor tear, Lirien's open surveillance

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Defensive calculation with suppressed competence reawakening — the tactical mind refusing to stay dormant, anger at being watched bleeding through resignation
CHAR-001.trigger: Lirien's refusal to pretend surveillance isn't happening, Pallor anomalies increasing

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Controlled paranoia validated — institutional betrayal confirmed through Tavik's compromise, the Calwrath wound reopening as trusted systems prove untrustworthy again
CHAR-001.trigger: Tavik's transparent fear, realization that Conclave still monitors her
CHAR-001.actualState: Bitter vindication transitioning to reluctant pragmatism — controlled paranoia validated through conspiracy discovery, forced into alliance with Lirien out of survival necessity not trust
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Hands shake only after leaving Tavik office (suppressed emotional response). Cold blade-sharp clarity during message reading (tactical focus). Admits to Lirien: I do not trust you. Lirien: You should not. Accepts alliance anyway because survival requires it.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Reluctant tactical engagement — forced to use skills she buried, Calwrath memories surfacing as external threats require command-grade assessment
CHAR-001.trigger: Conclave-linked threat in Duskhaven, need to assess Lirien as ally versus threat

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Forced alliance acceptance with undercurrent of self-loathing — choosing survival over isolation, hating that she still makes calculations this cold
CHAR-001.trigger: Quietus paradox revealed, staying in Duskhaven becomes impossible
CHAR-001.actualState: Pragmatic bitterness transitioning to grim resolve — archives proof confirms institutional betrayal, accepts cooperation as existential necessity not trust, applies Calwrath arithmetic to world-scale stakes
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Compressed reaction to kill contract (hands shake only after Lirien leaves). Calwrath arithmetic applied: world coherence > personal safety. Final scene: packs methodically, commits to departure despite distrust. I trusted arithmetic. And the numbers said: go.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Departure as simultaneous loss and liberation — leaving the cage she built, uncertain if partnership with Lirien is another form of captivity
CHAR-001.trigger: Forced to flee Duskhaven with Lirien, journey begins

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Grudging respect emerging through competence recognition — noticing Lirien's precision, finding it harder to maintain emotional distance under shared survival pressure
CHAR-001.trigger: Road travel, competence-based cooperation, Lirien's tactical excellence

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Vulnerability exposure with shame and nascent trust — can no longer hide the Oathbrand's damage, forced to accept help she doesn't believe she deserves
CHAR-001.trigger: Healer names the crisis plainly, Kaelith stops concealing deterioration

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Physical attraction registering as threat and temptation — desire intrudes before readiness, Calwrath guilt frames wanting as indulgence undeserved
CHAR-001.trigger: Proximity under Pallor damage, reliance on Lirien's presence for coherence

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Hope cautiously considered then suppressed — Legion survivor contact suggests vindication might be possible, but hope has betrayed her before
CHAR-001.trigger: Ashborne testimony, possibility of moral vindication

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Overwhelming vulnerability and connection — Lirien's hands on her collarbone, magic flooding back, the most intimate exposure since Calwrath's aftermath, terrifying and exhilarating
CHAR-001.trigger: Oathbrand removal, magic return, resonance bond formation

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Power reclaimed with identity uncertainty — magic returned means she is no longer just the woman who broke at Calwrath, but who she is WITH power remains unclear
CHAR-001.trigger: Adjusting to restored magic, Quietus path awakening in her body

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Trust offered as deliberate risk — sharing Calwrath's unsanitized truth, bracing for recoil, finding witness instead, restraint breaking into kiss
CHAR-001.trigger: Calwrath confession, Lirien's refusal to flinch, first kiss
CHAR-001.actualState: Defensive fear transitioning through raw exposure to terrified hope — bond exposes Calwrath agitation, chooses to confess unsanitized truth, hands shake through telling, Lirien witnesses without flinching, kiss as deliberate choice, hands steady in aftermath
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Hands shake during confession but steady after kiss. Tells Calwrath like a mission briefing with emotion leaking through cracks. 'If I had those eight minutes again, I would make the same call. And I hate that about myself.' Closes distance deliberately — 'This is not survival. This is not the bond. This is not necessity.' Forehead against Lirien's, breathing settling, hands no longer shaking.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Desire accepted without full surrender — physical intimacy beginning, letting herself want without yet believing she can keep this
CHAR-001.trigger: Combined competence survival, deepening physical intimacy
CHAR-001.actualState: Focused combat calm transitioning through tentative joy to cautious hope — bond-enabled combat synchronization, post-combat adrenaline breaks restraint into physical intimacy, Lirien touches fae-marked places with recognition. Asks about future (Vael Seren). Senses Lirien carrying unresolved weight but chooses not to demand specifics. Quietus pull stronger.
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Combat: hands steady, tactical decisions clean, bond synchronization natural. Post-combat: hand covers Lirien's on ribs, thumb traces knuckles. 'Inside' — single word carrying need. Intimacy: allows vulnerability sounds to escape military discipline. 'Someone I want tomorrows with' — first explicit statement of desire for shared future. Chooses not to press on Lirien's 'variables.'

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Dangerous hope that love and mission might align — the illusion of clean resolution, believing for the first time that trust was not a mistake
CHAR-001.trigger: Intimacy deepens, conspiracy evidence accumulating, false sense of progress

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Trust architecture collapsed — every shared moment recontextualized as operative manipulation. Bond walled but not severed. Departing alone into Narrowing treeline with military containment of grief.
CHAR-001.trigger: Intercepted Court communique reveals active kill contract with blood binding. Bond transmits Lirien's guilt before words confirm it. 'Every feeling was built on a lie' delivered in casualty-report register.
CHAR-001.openingState: Peaceful trust (asleep beside Lirien after ch15 declaration)
CHAR-001.closingState: Walking alone east into Narrowing. Bond deliberately walled. Every memory under revision.
CHAR-001.bondState: Walled — emotional bleed reduced to substrate hum

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Grim resolve with fragile hope — anger cracked by bond evidence of genuine grief. Walking toward Lirien framed as mission necessity. Grief replacing anger as dominant register. Self-deception about tactical framing acknowledged but maintained as protective fiction.
CHAR-001.trigger: Bond pulse proving Lirien's grief genuine, Pallor acceleration contracting timeline to days, vorakh fight exposing physical decline, tactical reality forcing direction change toward Lirien
CHAR-001.actualState: Controlled anger collapsed into complex grief. Simple betrayal narrative failed against bond evidence. Kaelith now holds dual truth: feelings were real AND contract was real. Walking north toward Quietus/Lirien convergence. Bond wall relaxed but not opened. Mission framing as excuse to return — the lie is transparent and she knows it.
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Stops reinforcing bond wall (SCN-48). Bond registers direction change (SCN-49). 'Personal considerations are irrelevant' — immediately identified as obvious lie. 'She was not ready. She walked anyway.' Final lines: walking north with two griefs carried across distance.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Walking north with grim resolve — Pallor accelerating, solo mission no longer viable. Bond transmits Lirien's grief shifting to intention. Kaelith feels the quality change but cannot interpret it fully.
CHAR-001.trigger: Continued Pallor progression, mission necessity, bond carrying Lirien's quality change
CHAR-001.actualState: Not POV in Ch18. State inferred from Ch17 ending: walking north with two griefs. Bond registers Lirien's shift from grief to intention at end of Ch18 — first positive signal. Kaelith's emotional response to this shift will be explored in Ch19.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Reunion earned through action — Lirien returns with proof of free choice, trust rebuilt on different foundation: not absence of threat but choice to remain vulnerable despite it
CHAR-001.trigger: Lirien's choice to pursue freely, shared plan formed with full disclosure
CHAR-001.actualState: Reunion earned through deliberate choice, not forgiveness. Kaelith names the fear she has never spoken ('choosing love will put me back in Calwrath'). Trust rebuilt as engineering — cracked foundations mapped and loaded accordingly. Bond opens fully for first time since black moment. Partnership restored through operational planning that doubles as trust test.
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Stays instead of fleeing (two exits identified, neither taken). 'Commander' hits like siege stone — isolation revealed as imposed. Names fear in undefended voice ('underneath the commander, underneath the garrison-coast irony, underneath every wall'). Decision framed as structural: 'cracked foundations could bear weight, if you understood where the fractures ran.' Bond opened deliberately. 'If I am trusting you with the world, I can trust you with this' — steel, not warmth. Final assessment: 'Sound enough.'
CHAR-001.bondState: Opened fully — deliberate, complete, first time since Ch16 black moment. Wall removed by choice. Carrying both of them — fear, resolve, damaged trust held with choice.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Trust as ultimate surrender — allowing Lirien to stop her heart, choosing vulnerability over safety, believing love can survive death for seconds
CHAR-001.trigger: Contract loophole execution, heart stopped and restarted, binding released
CHAR-001.actualState: Post-cardiac-arrest. Consented to heart stopping — ultimate vulnerability. Felt nothing during cessation (8 seconds). Woke to bond flooding back. Structural doubt about Lirien's love collapsed when binding dissolved and feelings persisted. Immediate redirect to mission — Quietus, Thalen, god-seal. But hand did not leave Lirien's. Fierce tenderness held in clipped military language.
CHAR-001.evidenceFromProse: Consent given in steady voice: 'I consent.' Hand finds Lirien's after resurrection — grips hard. 'The binding is gone. You're still here.' Redirects to Quietus immediately but forehead stays against Lirien's. Does not let go of hand.
CHAR-001.bondState: Open, full, cleaner. Binding interference removed. Resonance sharper than ever. No contract between them for the first time.

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Purpose reclaimed through chosen action — reaching the Quietus, using Warden inheritance, making terrible arithmetic serve protection rather than destruction
CHAR-001.trigger: Quietus approach, confronting Thalen, preventing Ythara's full waking

CHAR-001.emotionalState: Chosen life with weight carried — not absolved of Calwrath but no longer defined by it alone, loving and being loved without needing institutional permission
CHAR-001.trigger: Crisis contained, institutions confronted, remaining with Lirien after every external reason to do so has fallen away
CHAR-001.actualState: Deep, resting sleep after maximum exertion at Quietus. Wakes to a quietness that borders on stillness — load-bearing peace. Receives Conclave's hedge without anger or despair — exhales, releases, has outgrown the need. 'I stopped needing their permission when I started needing something real.' Discusses Rivenmarch future — allowing herself to want something that is not punishment. Receives Lirien's declaration with open stillness — hope no longer braced for loss. 'I know. I want it too.' Thumb tracing Lirien's knuckles — the gesture of a woman choosing to be present.

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Perfect operational calibration with first-time fear filed as data — the 44th contract, arriving with parameters, target blood in veins, fear processed but not felt
CHAR-002.trigger: Arriving in Duskhaven, beginning surveillance of Kaelith

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Operational recalibration as anomalies multiply — target more competent than file suggested, Oathbrand anomaly introducing uncertainty, the most dangerous variable
CHAR-002.trigger: Kaelith catches her watching, Oathbrand and Pallor anomalies noted

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Professional assessment complicated by unwanted respect — target is not what intelligence claimed, operative instinct says the contract parameters are incomplete
CHAR-002.trigger: Observing Kaelith's tactical competence, institutional compromise through Tavik
CHAR-002.actualState: Professional assessment transitioning to active protection — respect for Kaelith competence deepening, choosing to disrupt enforcer custody without being asked, revealing operative status as trust gesture
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Scaffolding collapse executed with precision. Admits Because I need you alive. Later clarifies: I was sent to assess you, execution was one possible outcome, no longer pursuing it. Guides escape with tactical competence.

CHAR-002.emotionalState: First doubt not as moral qualm but as mission design flaw — a poorly designed contract is deeper violation than a cruel one, professional disgust emerging
CHAR-002.trigger: Conclave threat suggesting contract purpose is more complex than stated

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Strategic catastrophe recognition — the paradox revealed, killing Kaelith destroys god-seal access, contract becomes operationally incoherent, loophole search begins as professional necessity
CHAR-002.trigger: Quietus paradox discovered, contract conflicts with strategic necessity

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Commitment to partnership as mission preservation — leaving Duskhaven together locks trajectory, can no longer execute cleanly, buying time to solve paradox
CHAR-002.trigger: Forced departure, partnership formalized through shared flight

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Competence recognition bleeding into something non-operational — noticing Kaelith's precision, finding tactical cooperation unexpectedly satisfying, emotional distance harder to maintain
CHAR-002.trigger: Road travel, forced proximity, competence-based cooperation

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Involuntary caring through fae resonance — sensing Kaelith's pain depth, feeling what Kaelith suppresses, intelligence-gathering breaking something in her
CHAR-002.trigger: Using fae resonance to sense Kaelith's hidden deterioration, healer naming crisis

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Structural failure beginning — caring that cannot be filed as operational, obligation the contract didn't account for, Kaelith no longer target profile but person in pain
CHAR-002.trigger: Ythara exposure forcing reliance on each other, emotional resonance deepening

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Loophole research as lifeline — if she can solve the paradox technically, maybe the attachment is still containable, maybe she hasn't completely failed her conditioning
CHAR-002.trigger: Researching contract language, seeking technical solution to preserve both mission and Kaelith

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Acknowledged attachment through intimacy threshold — hands on Kaelith's collarbone, dismantling the ward, resonance bond forming, can no longer pretend this is professional
CHAR-002.trigger: Oathbrand removal procedure, sustained physical intimacy, resonance bond formation

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Structural failure accepted as reality — the Court would call this damage, she calls it becoming, the framework was wrong about wholeness
CHAR-002.trigger: Resonance bond reveals attachment undeniable, choosing not to fight it

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Witness as intimacy — Kaelith shares Calwrath, Lirien refuses recoil, kiss breaks restraint, the first thing she does that cannot be defended as tactical
CHAR-002.trigger: Calwrath confession witnessed, first kiss, choosing vulnerability
CHAR-002.actualState: Steady witness transitioning to deliberate vulnerability — holds position during Calwrath confession, shares operation 31 as equivalent trust, initiates physical contact (hand on jaw), does not classify the kiss, does not retreat from it
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Does not leave during confession. 'You're not asking for forgiveness. You're asking if I flinch. I don't flinch.' Shares Vael'thrin operation and Elisi's name. Hand on Kaelith's jaw — 'the lightest pressure, contact so careful it was almost a question.' 'I can feel you' — the simplest admission.

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Desire no longer containable as strategy — physical intimacy beginning, body ceasing to be instrument of contract, becoming language for feelings not yet speakable
CHAR-002.trigger: Physical intimacy deepening, loophole research continuing
CHAR-002.actualState: Fierce focus transitioning through terrified bond-dependency to cautious surrender and guilty determination — combat reveals bond makes them a single weapon (and single vulnerability). Post-combat desire breaks professional containment. First full intimacy: surrenders control completely, finds it right. Guilt arrives like tidewater during post-intimacy conversation. Discovers contract loophole. Commits to saving Kaelith through technical fulfillment without confession.
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Combat: 'Three people in the treeline' — field-note precision. Feels Kaelith's rib impact through bond, shoves through phantom pain. Mid-combat: 'She had just staked her survival on a woman she was contractually bound to kill.' Intimacy: 'surrendered control completely and it felt right.' Post-intimacy on Kaelith's collarbone: heartbeat 'steady and real and hers.' Guilt: 'tidewater — not crashing, not dramatic. Seeping.' Loophole: 'Cessation was a state, not a duration.' Final kiss on shoulder: 'a declaration of war — not against her, but against the terms of her death.'

CHAR-002.emotionalState: False hope that love and contract might both be kept — believing she can solve this cleanly, that precision can protect what matters
CHAR-002.trigger: Intimacy scene, conspiracy progress suggesting resolution possible

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Existential paralysis — cannot follow Kaelith because following might be binding compliance, not choice. Self-recognition as the instrument of betrayal. Identity-level crisis about whether desire is genuine or Court-designed.
CHAR-002.trigger: Intercepted Seep communique reveals active enforcement, blood binding, 47-day overdue status. Bond transmits distress before she can mask it. Hands message to Kaelith because concealment is already another lie.
CHAR-002.openingState: Pre-dawn operational check. Holding the impossible fact of last night's declaration.
CHAR-002.closingState: Motionless in doorway. Cannot determine if desire to follow is love or contract. Bond carries matched grief across distance.
CHAR-002.bondState: Walled by Kaelith — Lirien feels the absence as frozen river

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Raw grief transmitted through bond — uncontrolled, patient, the permanent weight of having lost something irreplaceable. Not diminishing. Quality of loss not mission failure but personal devastation.
CHAR-002.trigger: Kaelith's departure, bond walling, inability to determine if desire to follow is love or binding compliance
CHAR-002.actualState: Perceived through Kaelith's bond reception only (not POV). Grief described as vast, uncontrolled, structure-failing. No operative precision. Bond pulse in the night — involuntary. Gains edge of alertness when Kaelith changes direction north. Waiting but not passively.
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Bond carries 'the particular quality of someone who had lost something irreplaceable and knew they would never get it back.' Grief 'had taken on a quality of something that would last — not a spike but a new permanent feature.' Direction change perceived: 'the quality of Lirien's grief changing, gaining an edge of something that might have been alertness.'

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Hard-won clarity after dark night of the soul — agency proved through Oathbrand analysis, cipher destroyed as identity severance, free choice to follow Kaelith. Not hope — something harder. The particular clarity that came after the worst of an analysis was finished.
CHAR-002.trigger: Oathbrand removal classified as free choice (one verified act of defiance), Ashborne intelligence providing actionable path forward, cipher destruction severing operative identity
CHAR-002.actualState: Moved from existential paralysis (Ch16 intensity 10) through analytical death-spiral to breakthrough. The eleven-hour self-interrogation produced one verified data point: free choice is possible within the binding. Not certainty — 'cured permanently of certainty' — but clarity. Cipher destruction represents voluntary identity death. Walking north is the first free action since the communiqué. Bond carries intention (not grief) for the first time since separation.
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Classification system structures the entire chapter. Analytical death-spiral in SCN-50 resolved by one 'dry surface' of verified free choice. 'She could build from that.' SCN-52 closing: 'She was choosing the love.' Bond registers intention, not grief. Final line: 'She walked north.'

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Reunion earned through transparency — offering full disclosure, plan built on shared truth, no more omissions, proving choice can survive revelation
CHAR-002.trigger: Reuniting with Kaelith, sharing complete plan including loophole exploitation
CHAR-002.actualState: Arrives with proof: destroyed cipher, Ashborne intelligence, one verified free choice. Presents everything without operative framing — lays materials for inspection. Accepts that silence was hers, not the contract's. 'Then we burn together' — the response that earns Kaelith's decision. Relief when bond opens fully.
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Hands deliberately empty. Face 'open in a way Kaelith had never seen.' Does not close 10-pace distance — lets Kaelith decide. 'The silence was mine. That was not the contract. That was me.' Accepts shame as load-bearing. Bond carries relief + fear + 'something fiercer' when Kaelith agrees to the plan.

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Love proven through precision used for life not death — stopping Kaelith's heart to fulfill letter of contract, restarting it to prove trust survived, the cage opens
CHAR-002.trigger: Executing contract loophole, heart stopped and restarted, binding released
CHAR-002.actualState: Terrified precision through preparation. Felt Kaelith die through the bond — 8 seconds of devastating silence. Resuscitated through sheer will, magic, and love. Catalogued binding absence with operative thoroughness. Discovered feelings unchanged — empirical proof love is real. Tears unclassified for the first time. Surface and depth aligned when speaking to Kaelith.
CHAR-002.evidenceFromProse: Classification system structures preparation but fails during bond silence. 'The difference between a room gone dark and a room that had never contained light.' Analytical body-check post-dissolution: 'This was: evidence. This was also: proof.' 'I was always going to be here' — spoken without clinical register for the first time. Tears noted but not classified.
CHAR-002.bondState: Open, full, cleaner. Binding static removed. Bond is 'a signal without static.' Feels Kaelith's heartbeat as clearly as her own.

CHAR-002.emotionalState: Liberated precision turned to protection — fae skill used for Kaelith's survival rather than Court's enforcement, choosing Kaelith over civilization
CHAR-002.trigger: Supporting Kaelith at Quietus, precision enabling seal reinforcement

CHAR-002.emotionalState: First real choice after binding released — can return to fae territory or stay with Kaelith, chooses Kaelith not because she has to but because she wants to, wanting as revolution
CHAR-002.trigger: Standing at boundary with option to cross, choosing Kaelith freely
CHAR-002.actualState: Wakes to binding absence — first morning of genuine freedom. Catalogues changed world with intelligence precision but cannot classify her own emotional state ('unclassified'). Processes Conclave's institutional cowardice with pragmatic care for Kaelith. Observes Kaelith outgrow the need for vindication — a moment of tender recognition. In the quiet after crisis, confesses she does not know what to do — the first honest vulnerability without operational framing. Chooses Rivenmarch, chooses usefulness, chooses Kaelith: 'I stay because I want to.' The river finds its own shape.

notes: Initial emotional throughline populated from character arcs, romance stages, and chapter outlines. Both characters show emotional variety across 22 chapters with no violations of INV-037 (no same dominant emotion for 3+ consecutive chapters). Peak intensity moments: Ch11 (Oathbrand removal, 9-9), Ch16 (black moment, 10-10), Ch20 (contract fulfillment, 10-10), Ch21 (climax, 9-9). Lowest points: Ch1 (opening resignation, 4-4). Emotional progression follows dual-arc structure: personal stakes → relational stakes → existential stakes, with both characters moving from controlled precision/resignation through vulnerability and crisis to chosen connection.
